name: Install only recommended patches, restart if required (requires 10.13.4 or newer)
Install all available patches, restart if required (requires 10.13.4 or newer)
Proxmox - Get Guest Info
description: This script gets the status and basic info of all Proxmox guests on a host and saves it to a WYSIWYG custom field.

scriptVariables.name: Scott
scriptVariables.description: The name of the WYSIWYG custom field to save the VM info to.

name: Local Admins Report
description: Updates a custom field with a list of local admins.

scriptVariables.name: Custom Field Name
scriptVariables.description: Updates the custom field you specified (defaults to 'LocalAdmins'). The Custom Field needs to be writable by scripts (otherwise the script will report it as not found).

scriptVariables.name: Delimiter
scriptVariables.description: Places whatever is entered encased of quotes between each user name. Example: ' - ' will output: Local Admins Found: Administrator - adminuser1 - TEST\Domain Admins

name: Remove Microsoft Bloatware
description: Removes common bloatware that is often pre-installed on a PC.

scriptVariables.name: Apps To Remove
scriptVariables.description: A comma-separated list of Appx package names you would like to remove.
scriptVariables.defaultValue: Amazon.com.Amazon, AmazonVideo.PrimeVideo, Clipchamp.Clipchamp, Disney.37853FC22B2CE, DropboxInc.Dropbox, Facebook.Facebook, Facebook.InstagramBeta, king.com.BubbleWitch3Saga, king.com.CandyCrushSaga, king.com.CandyCrushSodaSaga, 5A894077.McAfeeSecurity, 4DF9E0F8.Netflix, SpotifyAB.SpotifyMusic, BytedancePte.Ltd.TikTok, 5319275A.WhatsAppDesktop

scriptVariables.name: Override With Custom Field Name
scriptVariables.description: Name of a multiline custom field to retrieve the 'Apps To Remove' list.

name: Chocolatey Install, Upgrade and Uninstall
description: This script allows you to install, uninstall, or upgrade an application using Chocolatey. If Chocolatey is not installed or is outdated, options are available to install or upgrade it before proceeding with the application action.

scriptVariables.name: Action
scriptVariables.description: Specify the action you would like to perform using chocolatey.

scriptVariables.name: Package Name
scriptVariables.description: Name of the application you would like to uninstall, upgrade, or install. https://community.chocolatey.org/packages is a good resource to find this.

scriptVariables.name: Version
scriptVariables.description: Optionally, specify a version to install.

scriptVariables.name: Allow Downgrades
scriptVariables.description: Allows downgrading existing installations to the specified version.

scriptVariables.name: Install Chocolatey If Necessary
scriptVariables.description: If Chocolatey isn't installed, this option installs it before starting your action.

scriptVariables.name: Upgrade Chocolatey
scriptVariables.description: If an update for Chocolatey itself is available, this option upgrades it to the latest version.

scriptVariables.name: Skip Sleep
scriptVariables.description: The script waits for a random interval between 1 and 15 minutes before performing an action with Chocolatey to help avoid rate limiting. Use this option to skip the wait. For more information, see https://docs.chocolatey.org/en-us/community-repository/community-packages-disclaimer#excessive-use.

name: (NLT Digital) - Enable Patch Pilot
description: This script checks for the custom field "Patch Pilot" on the device.  It then checks the policy applied and updates as required.

name: Set Excel Default Format to .xlsx
description: Set Excel Default Format to .xlsx

name: Set Default Doc format to docx
description: Set Default Doc format to docx

name: NLT License Check
description: NLT License Check

name: Set Folder Permissions (Everyone Group)
description: Granted "Everyone" permissions to the named folder

scriptVariables.name: FolderPath
scriptVariables.description: The path to be updated

name: Change Power and Sleep Settings
description:
Set Power and Sleep Settings. It can adjust just the plugged in or battery settings if requested.
Please Note not all devices support all options.
Options: ScreenTimeout, HibernateTimeout, SleepTimeout, Disk Timeout, PowerPlan, Lid Action, Wake Timers, USB Suspend, Critical Action
Low Action, Power Button Action, Critical Level, Low Level, Low Notification, and Critical Notification.

scriptVariables.name: Power Source Setting
scriptVariables.description: Should these changes affect the battery or plugged in settings of the power plan?

scriptVariables.name: Screen Timeout in Minutes
scriptVariables.description: Time in minutes to wait before turning off the screen. (0 to disable)

scriptVariables.name: Hibernate Timeout in Minutes
scriptVariables.description: Time in minutes to wait before hibernating the computer. (0 to disable)

scriptVariables.name: Sleep Timeout in Minutes
scriptVariables.description: Time in minutes to wait before the computer sleeps. (0 to disable)

scriptVariables.name: Disk Timeout in Minutes
scriptVariables.description: Time in minutes for the disk to spin down on idle. (0 to disable)

scriptVariables.name: Power Plan
scriptVariables.description: The desired power plan to switch to.

scriptVariables.name: Lid Action
scriptVariables.description: Action Windows should take when the laptop lid is closed.

scriptVariables.name: Power Button Action
scriptVariables.description: Action to take when the power button is pressed.

scriptVariables.name: Wake Timers
scriptVariables.description: Allows the ability for software to wake the computer from sleep at a later date.

scriptVariables.name: USB Suspend
scriptVariables.description: USB Auto Suspend allows a USB device to enter a low-power state when not in active use, conserving energy.

scriptVariables.name: Critical Battery Action
scriptVariables.description: Action to take when the battery is at critical levels.

scriptVariables.name: Low Battery Action
scriptVariables.description: Action to take when the battery is at low levels.

scriptVariables.name: Critical Battery Level
scriptVariables.description: A number without the percent sign representing what the critical battery threshold is. e.g. 7 is 7 percent full.

scriptVariables.name: Low Battery Level
scriptVariables.description: A number without the percent sign representing what the low battery threshold is. e.g. 10 is 10 percent full.

scriptVariables.name: Low Notification
scriptVariables.description: Should Windows alert the user if the battery reaches the low battery level?

scriptVariables.name: Critical Notification
scriptVariables.description: Should Windows alert the user if the battery reaches the critical battery level?

name: Disable Weak TLS and SSL Protocols
description: Disables TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1, SSL 2.0, SSL 3.0. Enables TLS 1.2.

scriptVariables.name: Force Restart
scriptVariables.description: Restarts the computer on script completion.

name: Install Office 365 Suite
description: Installs Office 365 from a config file or creates a generic config file and installs.

scriptVariables.name: Link To Configuration XML
scriptVariables.description: A link to a configuration file you would like to use. You can generate one using https://config.office.com/deploymentsettings .

scriptVariables.name: Restart Computer
scriptVariables.description: Should the script restart the machine on completion? Note that this will only happen if the install is successful.

name: Add URL to Site Zone
description:
Adds a specified website to Zones:
1      Local Intranet Zone
2      Trusted sites Zone
3      Internet Zone
4      Restricted Sites Zone

scriptVariables.name: URL
scriptVariables.description:
The URL that you want to add
E.g. https://zahezone.com.au

scriptVariables.name: Zone
scriptVariables.description:
What Zone do you wish to add it too:
1      Local Intranet Zone
2      Trusted sites Zone
3      Internet Zone
4      Restricted Sites Zone

name: Google Configuration - Notification Allowed URLS
description:
Nominates the URLS that are allowed to show notifications:
Sets Registry Key:
HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Policies\Google\Chrome\NotificationsAllowedForUrls

name: Google Configuration - Install Extensions
description:
Nominates Extensions to be installed within Google Chrome.
Updates Registry Key:
HKLM:\Software\Policies\Google\Chrome\ExtensionInstallForcelist

name: Google Configuration - Basic Setup
description: Sets Default Google Chrome Settings

name: Set Default Browser
description: Sets the default browser for all users.

scriptVariables.name: Action
scriptVariables.description: Specify whether you would like to set the default browser, or enable or disable the block on browser protocols and file types.

scriptVariables.name: Browser
scriptVariables.description: Select your desired default browser.

scriptVariables.name: Restart Explorer
scriptVariables.description: This will restart explorer.exe. This is only required to refresh the desktop icons for .html files (they will still open in the default you set).

scriptVariables.name: Force Restart Computer
scriptVariables.description: In order for the User Protection Driver updates to take immediate effect, you may need to restart the computer.

name: Deploy Wi-Fi Profile
description: Deploy a wifi profile to all users on a given device.

scriptVariables.name: SSID
scriptVariables.description: Specify the Wi-Fi SSID/name.

scriptVariables.name: Authentication Type
scriptVariables.description: Select either WPA2 authentication or WPA3.

scriptVariables.name: PreShared Key
scriptVariables.description: Enter the Pre-Shared Key of the Wifi

scriptVariables.name: Overwrite
scriptVariables.description: If the profile already exists overwrite it.

name: Enable or Disable New Outlook Forced Migration
description: Sets the required registry key to either block the forced migration from classic Outlook to New Outlook and optionally hides the Try the New Outlook toggle button.

scriptVariables.name: Migration Action
scriptVariables.description: Sets a policy to either disable or enable the automatic forced migration of classic Outlook to New Outlook.

scriptVariables.name: Toggle Button Action
scriptVariables.description: Hide or show the toggle button to migrate to the new outlook.

scriptVariables.name: Username to Set Policy For
scriptVariables.description: The username you would like to set the policy for. Leave blank for all users.

name: Set Lock Screen Wallpaper
description: Set the wallpaper for either an individual user (if run as Current Logged on User) or for all users with modifications disabled (if run as System). Set Image and Disable Modifications will set the lockscreen wallpaper and prevent end-users from changing their lockscreen image.

scriptVariables.name: Action
scriptVariables.description: Specify an action to perform on the lock screen. 'Set Image' requires the script to be run as 'Current Logged on User'. 'Set Image and Disable Modifications' will set the lockscreen and prevent end-users from changing their lockscreen image.

scriptVariables.name: URL or Local Path to Image
scriptVariables.description: Specify either a link or a local path to an image you would like to use as the lock screen background.

scriptVariables.name: Wallpaper Storage Path
scriptVariables.description: Specify a folder to store the wallpaper in. Defaults to C:\ProgramData\Lockscreen.

scriptVariables.name: Force Restart
scriptVariables.description: If no one is signed into the machine, a restart may be required for the script to take immediate effect.

name: Set Desktop Wallpaper
description: This sets the desktop background for all existing users (if run as System) or the currently logged-in user (if run as Current Logged on User). To have the wallpaper change take effect immediately, please select Replace Transcoded Wallpaper File and Restart Explorer.

scriptVariables.name: Wallpaper File
scriptVariables.description: A URL link or file path to your desired wallpaper.

scriptVariables.name: Directory to Store Wallpaper In
scriptVariables.description: Path to store the wallpaper file (must be accessible by user accounts using the wallpaper). Example: C:\ProgramData\Wallpaper

scriptVariables.name: Wallpaper Display Mode
scriptVariables.description: This option sets how the wallpaper is displayed in Windows (described as 'Fit' in the Control Panel).

scriptVariables.name: Replace Transcoded Wallpaper File
scriptVariables.description: Replace the file %APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\Themes\TranscodedWallpaper. This file is generated whenever the wallpaper is changed and is required for the wallpaper change to take immediate effect.

scriptVariables.name: Restart Explorer
scriptVariables.description: Restart explorer.exe. This is required for the wallpaper change to take immediate effect.

name: Set News and Interests
description: Hides or shows the News and Interests tab in the taskbar. On Windows 11, it hides or shows the widgets tab.

scriptVariables.name: Show Or Hide
scriptVariables.description: Should you hide or show 'News and Interests' in the taskbar?

scriptVariables.name: Prevent Changes
scriptVariables.description: Should the end-user be able to modify this setting after it has been set with this script?

scriptVariables.name: Restart Explorer
scriptVariables.description: In order for this script to take immediate effect explorer.exe will need to be restarted.

name: Move OST File Location to Documents
description: This will move the default OST file location to Documents. To be used on new Deployments.

name: Start OneDrive when user logs in
Silent Config OneDrive
description: This script checks if OneDrive is installed and running, and then configures it to silently sign in the current user using their Windows credentials.

name: Initial OneDrive Setup
description:
Prerequisites:
User signed in with MS account. 
OneDrive installed (V19 or later).

name: Time Sync - Configure Settings
description: Set the time zone and NTP time sync settings for the local machine.

scriptVariables.name: List Time Zones
scriptVariables.description: List the available time zones. If selected, all other options will be ignored.

scriptVariables.name: Set Time Zone
scriptVariables.description: Choose a time zone to set. Requires the display name from the 'List Time Zones' option.

scriptVariables.name: Enable and Start Windows Time Service
scriptVariables.description: Enable and start the Windows Time service if it is disabled or not running. This will set the Start Type to 'Automatic.' This service is a prerequisite for all time sync settings besides the time zone.

scriptVariables.name: Set Sync Type
scriptVariables.description: Choose to sync from NTP servers, from the domain, or both.
valueList: Both - Prefer Domain, NTP Servers As Fallback

scriptVariables.name: Set Sync Servers
scriptVariables.description: Specify NTP servers to sync from. Only used if 'Both' or 'NTP' is selected for Set Sync Type. Separate multiple servers with a comma.

scriptVariables.name: Sync Interval In Minutes
scriptVariables.description: Specify the sync interval in minutes. This value is required if using either 'Both' or 'NTP' for Set Sync Type, and only applies to NTP syncing. Valid range is 1 - 546 minutes.

scriptVariables.name: Enable or Disable Guest VM to Host Sync
scriptVariables.description: For virtual machines, enable or disable time synchronization from the guest to the host. Setting this to 'Disable' is required for the other time sync settings to work as expected.

scriptVariables.name: Sync Now
scriptVariables.description: Force a sync after the settings are applied.

name: Active Directory - Join Computer to a Domain
description: Joins a computer to a domain.

scriptVariables.name: Domain To Join
scriptVariables.description: Domain to join the computer to.

scriptVariables.name: Username To Join Domain With
scriptVariables.description: The username to join domain with. E.g. 'DOMAIN\username'

scriptVariables.name: Password To Join Domain With Custom Field
scriptVariables.description: Gets the password to join domain with, from a secure custom field.

scriptVariables.name: Server Name
scriptVariables.description: Used only when computer can't locate a domain controller via DNS or you wish to connect to a specific DC.

scriptVariables.name: No Restart
scriptVariables.description: Do not restart computer after joining to a domain.

name: Rename Computer
description: Rename PC with company name and machine serial.

name: Set region and timezone
description: Set the region and time zone to Australia S. Eastern Time (UTC +10)

name: Rename and Domain join
description:
Variable format:
MyCompany
corp.mycompany.local
OU=Workstations,DC=corp,DC=mycompany,DC=local
corp\\domainjoinuser
Secure password stored in NinjaRMM

name: Install network printer
description:
File transfer drivers to C:\printerdrivers
$DriverName must match INF file name

scriptVariables.name: DriverName
scriptVariables.description: Must match INF file name

name: DEX Survey - Script 1 - Request User Feedback
description: Script 1 of 3 in the DEX Survey experience. Displays a popup on the users screen requesting feedback on any performance issues they might be experiencing. It must be run as the Current Logged-on User.

scriptVariables.name: Application ID
scriptVariables.description: This will replace the window title from your Ninja company name to the specified value.

scriptVariables.name: Logo Path
scriptVariables.description: The location of an image you would like to use in the large image window. E.g., https://www.google.com/favicon.ico or C:\ProgramData\logo.png. The image window has a maximum size of 150px x 250px.

scriptVariables.name: Icon Path
scriptVariables.description: The location of an image you would like to use in the taskbar and window's title bar. E.g., https://www.google.com/favicon.ico or C:\ProgramData\icon.png. Taskbar and window icons are traditionally 32px x 32px.

scriptVariables.name: PopUp Message
scriptVariables.description: The text you want to appear in the popup message.
scriptVariables.defaultValue: We've detected your computer might be performing slowly. Are you currently experiencing any performance issues?

scriptVariables.name: Prompt Frequency
scriptVariables.description: Sets the frequency in minutes at which the condition is allowed to trigger. The user will only be prompted if the prompt has not been displayed within that time period.

name: DEX Survey - Script 2 - Collect User Feedback
description: Script 2 of 3 in the DEX Survey experience. Saves the latest feedback from DEX Survey - Script 1 to the multiline custom field Latest User Feedback and optionally stores feedback history in the WYSIWYG field User Feedback History.

scriptVariables.name: Keep Feedback History
scriptVariables.description: This stores the user feedback history in a custom field titled 'User Feedback History'.

scriptVariables.name: Max Comments to Keep
scriptVariables.description: This controls the number of feedback comments to retain in the log file(s).

name: DEX Survey - Script 3 - System Performance Check
description: Script 3 of 3 in the DEX Survey experience. Based on the feedback in Script 1, collects system performance data (CPU, memory, disk, and network). The results can optionally be saved to a WYSIWYG custom field.

scriptVariables.name: Display User Message
scriptVariables.description: Display a message to the end-user informing them that you are collecting performance metrics and that they should not restart the computer.

scriptVariables.name: Days Since Last Reboot
scriptVariables.description: Specify the number of days by which the system should have been rebooted.

scriptVariables.name: Duration to Perform Tests
scriptVariables.description: The duration (in minutes) for which the performance tests should be executed.

scriptVariables.name: Number of Events
scriptVariables.description: The number of error events to retrieve from the Application, Security, Setup, and System event logs.

scriptVariables.name: WYSIWYG Custom Field Name
scriptVariables.description: Optionally specify the name of a WYSIWYG custom field to store the formatted performance data.

name: Device Uptime Percentage Monitor
description: Get the Uptime percentage of a Windows device and show a list of boot events.

scriptVariables.name: Uptime for the Past X Days
scriptVariables.description: Number of days to look back in time to calculate uptime %. This cannot be used with Start Day and End Day.

scriptVariables.name: Start Day
scriptVariables.description: The start date for calculating the uptime %. This can only be used with End Day.

scriptVariables.name: End Day
scriptVariables.description: The end date for calculating the uptime %. This can only be used with Start Day.

scriptVariables.name: Estimate Unexpected Shutdown Time
scriptVariables.description: Estimates the shutdown time for an unexpected shutdown using the last event log entry.

scriptVariables.name: WYSIWYG Custom Field Name
scriptVariables.description: The name of a WYSIWYG custom field to save the report to.

scriptVariables.name: Percentage Custom Field Name
scriptVariables.description: The name of a text custom field to save just the uptime percentage to.

name: System Performance Check
description: Collects system performance data (CPU, memory, disk, and network). The results can optionally be saved to a WYSIWYG custom field.

scriptVariables.name: Display User Message
scriptVariables.description: Display a message to the end-user informing them that you are collecting performance metrics and that they should not restart the computer.

scriptVariables.name: Days Since Last Reboot
scriptVariables.description: Specify the number of days by which the system should have been rebooted.

scriptVariables.name: Duration to Perform Tests
scriptVariables.description: The duration (in minutes) for which the performance tests should be executed.

scriptVariables.name: Number of Events
scriptVariables.description: The number of error events to retrieve from the Application, Security, Setup, and System event logs.

scriptVariables.name: Perform Internet Speed Test
scriptVariables.description: Determines whether to perform an internet speed test as part of the system performance assessment. This test uses M-Lab's MSAK server under their Acceptable Use Policy. By proceeding, you consent to public collection of network data, including your IP.

scriptVariables.name: Random Sleep for Speed Test
scriptVariables.description: Sleep for a random duration between 0 and 60 minutes before running the Speedtest.

scriptVariables.name: WYSIWYG Custom Field Name
scriptVariables.description: Optionally specify the name of a WYSIWYG custom field to store the formatted performance data.

name: Display PopUp
description: Displays a popup on the end users screen. The script needs to be run as the Current Logged In User. Use Restart Reminder to display a request to the end user to restart their computer. See the script code to save your icon and logo as Base64.

scriptVariables.name: Restart Reminder
scriptVariables.description: A shortcut to set multiple parameters suitable for a generic restart prompt, such as setting appropriate values for the button text, a countdown, and standard message text. See the script code for more details.

scriptVariables.name: Application ID
scriptVariables.description: This will replace the window title from your Ninja company name to the specified value.

scriptVariables.name: Logo Path
scriptVariables.description: The location of an image you would like to use in the large image window. E.g., https://www.google.com/favicon.ico or C:\ProgramData\logo.png. The image window has a maximum size of 150px x 250px.

scriptVariables.name: Icon Path
scriptVariables.description: The location of an image you would like to use in the taskbar and window's title bar. E.g., https://www.google.com/favicon.ico or C:\ProgramData\icon.png. Taskbar and window icons are traditionally 32px x 32px.

scriptVariables.name: PopUp Message
scriptVariables.description: The text you want to appear in the popup message.

scriptVariables.name: Default Button
scriptVariables.description: Choose a button to activate when the end-user presses Enter while the popup window is in focus.

scriptVariables.name: Right Button Text
scriptVariables.description: Replaces the text in the right button with your input.

scriptVariables.name: Right Button Action
scriptVariables.description: Input any CMD command, such as logoff.exe, to execute after the right button is clicked.

scriptVariables.name: Right Button Countdown
scriptVariables.description: Adds a countdown to the right button in seconds. At the end of the countdown, the button will auto-click itself.

scriptVariables.name: Left Button Text
scriptVariables.description: Replaces the text in the left button with your input.

scriptVariables.name: Left Button Action
scriptVariables.description: Input any CMD command, such as logoff.exe, to execute after the left button is clicked.

scriptVariables.name: Left Button Countdown
scriptVariables.description: Adds a countdown to the left button in seconds. At the end of the countdown, the button will auto-click itself.

name: Display Toast Message - Important Notifications
description: Sends an important toast notification to the currently signed in user. Please run as the Current Logged-on User.

scriptVariables.name: Title
scriptVariables.description: The Title used in the Toast notification. Maximum length displayed is 42 characters. Any thing beyond the 42 characters will be truncated and not displayed.

scriptVariables.name: Message
scriptVariables.description: The Message used in the Toast notification. Maximum length displayed is 254 characters, but the toast can be expanded to show more of the message, but will not show the full message if it is too long.

scriptVariables.name: Application Id
scriptVariables.description: The Application Id that this script will use, defaults to your company name. This is not visible to the user.

name: Popup - Computer Name
description: Pops up with the computer naem

description: Displays a popup on the end users screen. The script needs to be run as the Current Logged In User. Use Restart Reminder to display a request to the end user to restart their computer. See the script code to save your icon and logo as Base64.

scriptVariables.name: PopUp Message

scriptVariables.name: Right Button Text
scriptVariables.description: Replaces the text in the right button with your input.

scriptVariables.name: Right Button Action
scriptVariables.description: Input any CMD command, such as logoff.exe, to execute after the right button is clicked.

scriptVariables.name: Right Button Countdown
scriptVariables.description: Adds a countdown to the right button in seconds. At the end of the countdown, the button will auto-click itself.

name: Remove All Older Kernel packages without warning
description:
dnf remove: Removes packages.
-y: Skips confirmation prompts.
$(dnf repoquery --installonly --latest-limit=-1): Lists all install-only packages except the latest version (typically old kernels).

name: Deploy Latest Windows 11 Feature Update
description: Update Windows 11 to the latest available Feature Update using the Windows 11 Install Assistant. This script does not wait for the installation assistant to complete.

name: Force Update
description: Force Update

name: Invoke-NinjaDiskCleanup
description: Cleans Up Drive and inputs fields back into device.

name: Sync Mosyle Device to Halo
description: This script sync's Mosyle device to Halo to ensure accurate billing and device management.

scriptVariables.name: mosylepassword_b64
scriptVariables.description:
MUST be Base 65
$pw = Read-Host "Password" -AsSecureString
$bstr = [Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::SecureStringToBSTR($pw)
$plain = [Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::PtrToStringBSTR($bstr)
[Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::ZeroFreeBSTR($bstr)
[Convert]::ToBase64String([Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($plain))

name: Install Claude Desktop
description: Install Claude Desktop
